steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a subject-verb agreement question. Find the grammatical subject of the blank, determine its number (singular or plural), and pick the verb form that matches. Ignore any intervening phrases set off by dashes.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'have become': The subject is "lost works" (plural). The parenthetical phrase "no copy of which exists" is set off by dashes and does not change the subject. "Have become" is the plural present-perfect form and agrees with "lost works." ✓
  • Choice B — 'becomes': Third-person singular simple present. Agrees with a singular subject, not with the plural "lost works." ✗
  • Choice C — 'has become': Third-person singular present perfect. Also requires a singular subject. ✗
  • Choice D — 'is becoming': Third-person singular present progressive. Does not agree with the plural "lost works." ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
